steps.title: Find the ordered roots
steps.content:
Use the quadratic formula for each original equation. The first equation has roots
$$-4\pm \sqrt{3}$$
Thus $r=-4-\sqrt{3}$ and $s=-4+\sqrt{3}$. The second equation has roots
$$-6\pm 2\sqrt{3}$$
Thus $t=-6-2\sqrt{3}$ and $u=-6+2\sqrt{3}$.

steps.title: Form the new roots
steps.content:
The roots of the new equation are $r+u$ and $s+t$.
$$r+u=(-4-\sqrt{3})+(-6+2\sqrt{3})=-10+\sqrt{3}$$
$$s+t=(-4+\sqrt{3})+(-6-2\sqrt{3})=-10-\sqrt{3}$$

steps.title: Multiply the roots
steps.content:
For $x^2+20x+c=0$, the constant term is the product of the roots.
$$c=(-10+\sqrt{3})(-10-\sqrt{3})=100-3=97$$
The answer is 97.
